_meta.what_firm_does: Mercury provides banking and financial software for startups and growth companies. This role designs and scales learning across Customer Support: end-to-end enablement programs, learning metrics + time-to-proficiency, multi-format instructional content (live/video/micro-learning/SOPs), knowledge management, and cross-functional gap interventions.
_meta.jd_wants: 3+ yrs L&D / instructional design / enablement; manage programs planning→execution; data-driven measurement of learning effectiveness; instructional design + facilitation; systems thinking; LMS + design tools (WorkRamp, Articulate); knowledge mgmt (Guru, Notion).
_meta.status: DRAFT — strong fit. Jamie to review.

Why are you interested in this role?: What pulls me to this role is that it's L&D done right—not just publishing courses, but diagnosing where people actually struggle and building learning that changes behavior. At InGenius Prep I run 20+ enablement programs, facilitate training for 600+ people, and maintain the knowledge systems for 70+ staff. The part I love most is the diagnosis: when a rollout I owned didn't get adopted, I interviewed the users, found the real blocker was friction not resistance, and built a just-in-time asset that fixed it. A Customer Support Learning & Enablement role is exactly that instinct, focused on getting a support team to proficiency faster.
Why do you want to work at Mercury?: Two reasons. First, the work is genuinely the work I do best—designing multi-format learning, building knowledge systems, and measuring time-to-proficiency rather than just completion. Second, I appreciate that Mercury treats clarity and craft as a value; that maps onto how I think about enablement—make the path to competence obvious and well-documented. I'd be excited to own the learning experience for a support org where getting people accurate and confident quickly directly shapes how customers experience the company.
How do you measure whether training actu: I measure behavior, not completion. Completion rates tell you people clicked through; they don't tell you anything changed. At NextGen I analyzed 2,000+ employee experiences and let the data define the real driver before recommending anything. For support enablement specifically, I'd track time-to-proficiency, quality and accuracy of responses post-training, and whether people actually use the new resources—then go talk to the people who are still stuck rather than assuming. That diagnose-measure-iterate loop is how I cut onboarding time 75% at InGenius. AI helps here too: I use it daily and have been building a RAG-based system, so I think a lot about personalized, in-the-moment learning at scale.
Tell us about yourself / anything else: I'm an analytical organizer with a growth mindset, grounded in an MS in Applied Organizational Psychology and a BA in Psychology and Education Studies—so learning design is genuinely where my training points. In practice I diagnose with data where people fall short of proficiency, collaborate cross-functionally on the intervention, and track behavioral signals to make sure it sticks. I pick up LMS and authoring tools quickly and enjoy it. I'd love to bring that systematic, human-centered approach to your support team.
